title: Introduction to cosexpressionR
text: R open-source statistical environment can easily modified enhance functionality via packages. consexpressionR R package available via Bioconductor repository packages. R can installed operating system CRAN can install consexpressionR using following commands R session:
code: if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE)) {   install.packages("BiocManager") }  BiocManager::install("consexpressionR")  ## Check that you have a valid Bioconductor installation BiocManager::valid()

text: Costa-Silva, J., Menotti, D., Lopes, M. F. consexpressionR: R package consensus differential gene expression analysis arxiv (2025)
code: @Article{,   title = {consexpressionR: an R package for consensus differential gene expression analysis},   author = {Juliana Costa-Silva and David Menotti and Fabricio M. Lopes},   year = {2025},   journal = {arxiv},   doi = {10.48550/arXiv.2503.21546}, }

what: Differential Expression Analysis using consensus of 7 methods
title: Differential Expression Analysis using consensus of 7 methods
text: R package consexpressionR R package differential expression analysis count quantification data. just one count quantification file, user can obtain list genes indicated differentially expressed 5 methods. user-friendly graphical interface, can used web browser, user can parameterize 7 expression analysis methodologies. differential expression analysis performed methods edgeR, DeSeq2, SAMSeq, KnowSeq, EBSeq, limma, NOISeq. results tool can written .csv files. Visualization carried heat map Plot, containing genes considered differentially expressed 5 methods. consexpressionR R package differential expression analysis count quantification data. just one count quantification file, user can obtain list genes indicated differentially expressed 5 methods. user-friendly graphical interface, can used web browser, user can parameterize 7 expression analysis methodologies. differential expression analysis performed methods edgeR, DeSeq2, SAMSeq, KnowSeq, EBSeq, limma, NOISeq. results tool can written .csv files. Visualization carried radarPlot, containing genes thatwere considered differentially expressed 5 methods. can see pkgdown action https://costasilvati.shinyapps.io/consexpressionRapp/. Learn vignette("consexpressionR").

title: Differential Expression Analysis using consensus of 7 methods
code: # Install released version from github library(devtools) install_github("costasilvati/consexpressionR") library(consexpressionR)

title: Differential Expression Analysis using consensus of 7 methods
text: run shiny aplication view consexpressionR browser:
code: # Install released version from github library(devtools) install_github("costasilvati/consexpressionR") library(consexpressionR) # shinyApp running in localhost consexpressionR() library(consexpressionR) # shinyApp running in localhost consexpressionR()

text: S4 class store differential expression results

title: An S4 class to store differential expression results — ExpressionResultSet-class
text: results named list data.frames, corresponding method (e.g., edgeR, DESeq2, etc.) methodNames character vector methods used parameters list parameters used analysis consensus list containing consensus DEG results

title: An S4 class to store differential expression results — ExpressionResultSet-class
code: obj <- createExpressionResultSet(   results = list(edgeR = data.frame(gene = "g1", logFC = 1)),   methodNames = "edgeR",   parameters = list(),   consensus = list() ) obj #> ExpressionResultSet object #> Methods executed: edgeR #> Result tables: 1 #> Consensus: not computed or empty.

text: list.

title: Get consensus results — consensus
code: obj <- createExpressionResultSet(   results = list(edgeR = data.frame(gene = c("g1", "g2"), logFC = c(1, -1))),   methodNames = "edgeR",   consensus = list(g1 = TRUE) ) consensus(obj) #> $g1 #> [1] TRUE #>

code: consensusList(consexpressionList, deTool, threshold = 5)

title: consensusList: make a list of genes considered DE by at least 'threshold' tools — consensusList
text: consexpressionList ExpressionResultSet object containing results analysis method. deTool data.frame. Output 'listDeByTool' function threshold Integer. Minimum number tools must agree gene DE (default: 5)

title: consensusList: make a list of genes considered DE by at least 'threshold' tools — consensusList
text: ExpressionResultSet object consensus slot populated

title: consensusList: make a list of genes considered DE by at least 'threshold' tools — consensusList
code: set.seed(42) counts <- matrix(   as.integer(c(     rnbinom(200, mu = 10, size = 1),     rnbinom(200, mu = 100, size = 1)   )),   nrow = 100,   dimnames = list(     paste0("gene", seq_len(100)),     paste0("sample", seq_len(4))   ) ) groups_info <- c("control", "control", "treated", "treated") treats <- c("control", "treated") cons_result <- runExpression(   numberReplics = 2,   groupName = treats,   rDataFrameCount = counts,   controlDeseq2 = "control",   contrastDeseq2 = "treated" ) #> calcNormFactors has been renamed to normLibSizes #> Using classic mode. #> baySeq libsizes #> baySeq getPriors #> Finding priors... #> Warning: The '@replicates' slot is not a factor; converting now. #> done. #> Finding posterior likelihoods... #> Length of priorReps:0 #> Length of priorSubset:100 #> Length of subset:100 #> Length of postRows:100 #> Analysing part 1 of 1 #> Preparing data... #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> done. #> Estimating likelihoods... #> ...done! #> . #> done. #> Available columns in results_raw: #> Warning: ‘>=’ not meaningful for factors #> calcNormFactors has been renamed to normLibSizes #> Removing intercept from test coefficients #> [1] "Computing (M,D) values..." #> [1] "Computing probability of differential expression..." #> Warning: `expect_is()` was deprecated in the 3rd edition. #> ℹ Use `expect_type()`, `expect_s3_class()`, or `expect_s4_class()` instead #> Warning: `expect_is()` was deprecated in the 3rd edition. #> ℹ Use `expect_type()`, `expect_s3_class()`, or `expect_s4_class()` instead #> Warning: `expect_is()` was deprecated in the 3rd edition. #> ℹ Use `expect_type()`, `expect_s3_class()`, or `expect_s4_class()` instead #> Initial number of DE patterns = 2 #> Final number of DE patterns = 2 #> converting counts to integer mode #> estimating size factors #> estimating dispersions #> gene-wise dispersion estimates #> mean-dispersion relationship #> final dispersion estimates #> fitting model and testing #> Estimating sequencing depths... #> Resampling to get new data matrices... #> perm= 1 #> perm= 2 #> perm= 3 #> perm= 4 #> perm= 5 #> perm= 6 #> perm= 7 #> perm= 8 #> perm= 9 #> perm= 10 #> perm= 11 #> perm= 12 #> perm= 13 #> perm= 14 #> perm= 15 #> perm= 16 #> perm= 17 #> perm= 18 #> perm= 19 #> perm= 20 #> perm= 21 #> perm= 22 #> perm= 23 #> perm= 24 #> Number of thresholds chosen (all possible thresholds) = 11 #> Getting all the cutoffs for the thresholds... #> Getting number of false positives in the permutation... #> Differential expression analysis completed. expDef_result <- expressionDefinition(   resultTool = cons_result,   groups = treats ) deByTool <- listDeByTool(cons_result, row.names(counts), expDef_result) cons_result <- consensusList(cons_result, deByTool)  summary(cons_result) #> Summary of ExpressionResultSet #> --------------------------------- #> Methods used: edger, limma, noiseq, ebseq, deseq2, samseq #> Number of result tables: 6 #> Average number of DEGs per method: 86.83 #> Number of genes in consensus: 6

what: consexpressionR: Differential Expression Analysis using consensus of multiple methods — consexpressionR
title: consexpressionR: Differential Expression Analysis using consensus of multiple methods — consexpressionR
text: Comprehensive workflow differential expression (DE) analysis using multiple methods (e.g., DESeq2, edgeR, limma, EBSeq, NOISeq, SAMSeq, KnowSeq) consensus strategies derive robust DE gene lists.

title: consexpressionR: Differential Expression Analysis using consensus of multiple methods — consexpressionR
text: Maintainer: Juliana Costa-Silva julianacostasilvati@gmail.com (ORCID) [funder] Authors: Juliana Costa-Silva julianacostasilvati@gmail.com (ORCID) [funder] David Menotti menottid@gmail.com (ORCID) Fabricio M. Lopes fabricio@utfpr.edu.br (ORCID)

title: consexpressionR: Differential Expression Analysis using consensus of multiple methods — consexpressionR
code: obj <- createExpressionResultSet(   results = list(edgeR = data.frame(gene = "g1", logFC = 1)),   methodNames = "edgeR" ) obj #> ExpressionResultSet object #> Methods executed: edgeR #> Result tables: 1 #> Consensus: not computed or empty.

title: Constructor for ExpressionResultSet — createExpressionResultSet
text: Constructor ExpressionResultSet

title: Constructor for ExpressionResultSet — createExpressionResultSet
code: createExpressionResultSet(   results,   methodNames,   parameters = list(),   consensus = list() )

title: Constructor for ExpressionResultSet — createExpressionResultSet
text: results Named list data.frames (NULL elements). methodNames Character vector method names. parameters List analysis parameters. consensus List consensus results (default: empty list).

title: Constructor for ExpressionResultSet — createExpressionResultSet
text: ExpressionResultSet object.

title: Constructor for ExpressionResultSet — createExpressionResultSet
code: res_list <- list(   edgeR = data.frame(gene = c("g1", "g2"), logFC = c(1.2, -0.4)),   DESeq2 = NULL )  obj <- createExpressionResultSet(   results = res_list,   methodNames = c("edgeR", "DESeq2"),   parameters = list(alpha = 0.05),   consensus = list(g1 = TRUE) )  obj #> ExpressionResultSet object #> Methods executed: edgeR, DESeq2 #> Result tables: 2 #> Consensus: available (1 gene(s)) summary(obj) #> Summary of ExpressionResultSet #> --------------------------------- #> Methods used: edgeR, DESeq2 #> Number of result tables: 2 #> Average number of DEGs per method: 1.00 #> Number of genes in consensus: 1

what: Identify DE genes by method-specific thresholds — expressionDefinition
title: Identify DE genes by method-specific thresholds — expressionDefinition
text: Applies specific filtering rules expression method result ExpressionResultSet object returns DE genes per tool.

title: Identify DE genes by method-specific thresholds — expressionDefinition
code: expressionDefinition(   resultTool,   groups = c(""),   lfcMinLimma = -2,   lfcMaxLimma = 2,   pValueLimma = 0.05,   FLimma = 0.8,   lfcMinSamseq = -2,   lfcMaxSamseq = 2,   qValueSamseq = 0.05,   scoreDSamseq = 0.8,   lfcMinDeseq2 = -2,   lfcMaxDeseq2 = 2,   pValueDeseq2 = 0.05,   lfcMinEdger = -2,   lfcMaxEdger = 2,   pValueEdger = 0.05,   probNoiseq = 0.8,   lfcMaxKnowseq = 2,   lfcMinKnowseq = -2,   pValueKnowseq = 0.05,   deClassEbseq = "DE",   ppThresholdEbseq = 0.8,   printResults = FALSE,   pathOutput = "." )

title: Identify DE genes by method-specific thresholds — expressionDefinition
text: resultTool ExpressionResultSet object. groups Character vector group names conditions. lfcMinLimma Minimum logFC threshold limma. lfcMaxLimma Maximum logFC threshold limma. pValueLimma P-value cutoff limma. FLimma Minimum F-statistic multi-group limma. lfcMinSamseq Minimum fold-change SAMSeq. lfcMaxSamseq Maximum fold-change SAMSeq. qValueSamseq q-value cutoff SAMSeq. scoreDSamseq Score(d) threshold SAMSeq multi-class. lfcMinDeseq2 Minimum log2 fold-change DESeq2. lfcMaxDeseq2 Maximum log2 fold-change DESeq2. pValueDeseq2 P-value cutoff DESeq2. lfcMinEdger Minimum logFC edgeR. lfcMaxEdger Maximum logFC edgeR. pValueEdger P-value cutoff edgeR. probNoiseq Probability threshold NOISeq. lfcMaxKnowseq Maximum logFC KnowSeq. lfcMinKnowseq Minimum logFC KnowSeq. pValueKnowseq P-value cutoff KnowSeq. deClassEbseq DE class filter EBSeq (e.g. "DE" "EE"). ppThresholdEbseq Posterior probability threshold EBSeq. printResults Logical. Whether write results disk. pathOutput Character. Directory path save result tables.

title: Identify DE genes by method-specific thresholds — expressionDefinition
text: list data.frames DE genes per method.

title: Identify DE genes by method-specific thresholds — expressionDefinition
code: set.seed(42) counts <- matrix(   as.integer(c(     rnbinom(200, mu = 10, size = 1),     rnbinom(200, mu = 100, size = 1)   )),   nrow = 100,   dimnames = list(     paste0("gene", seq_len(100)),     paste0("sample", seq_len(4))   ) ) groups_info <- c("control", "control", "treated", "treated") treats <- c("control", "treated") res <- runExpression(   numberReplics = 2,   groupName = treats,   rDataFrameCount = counts,   controlDeseq2 = "control",   contrastDeseq2 = "treated" ) #> calcNormFactors has been renamed to normLibSizes #> Using classic mode. #> baySeq libsizes #> baySeq getPriors #> Finding priors... #> Warning: The '@replicates' slot is not a factor; converting now. #> done. #> Finding posterior likelihoods... #> Length of priorReps:0 #> Length of priorSubset:100 #> Length of subset:100 #> Length of postRows:100 #> Analysing part 1 of 1 #> Preparing data... #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> done. #> Estimating likelihoods... #> ...done! #> . #> done. #> Available columns in results_raw: #> Warning: ‘>=’ not meaningful for factors #> calcNormFactors has been renamed to normLibSizes #> Removing intercept from test coefficients #> [1] "Computing (M,D) values..." #> [1] "Computing probability of differential expression..." #> Warning: `expect_is()` was deprecated in the 3rd edition. #> ℹ Use `expect_type()`, `expect_s3_class()`, or `expect_s4_class()` instead #> Warning: `expect_is()` was deprecated in the 3rd edition. #> ℹ Use `expect_type()`, `expect_s3_class()`, or `expect_s4_class()` instead #> Warning: `expect_is()` was deprecated in the 3rd edition. #> ℹ Use `expect_type()`, `expect_s3_class()`, or `expect_s4_class()` instead #> Initial number of DE patterns = 2 #> Final number of DE patterns = 2 #> converting counts to integer mode #> estimating size factors #> estimating dispersions #> gene-wise dispersion estimates #> mean-dispersion relationship #> final dispersion estimates #> fitting model and testing #> Estimating sequencing depths... #> Resampling to get new data matrices... #> perm= 1 #> perm= 2 #> perm= 3 #> perm= 4 #> perm= 5 #> perm= 6 #> perm= 7 #> perm= 8 #> perm= 9 #> perm= 10 #> perm= 11 #> perm= 12 #> perm= 13 #> perm= 14 #> perm= 15 #> perm= 16 #> perm= 17 #> perm= 18 #> perm= 19 #> perm= 20 #> perm= 21 #> perm= 22 #> perm= 23 #> perm= 24 #> Number of thresholds chosen (all possible thresholds) = 11 #> Getting all the cutoffs for the thresholds... #> Getting number of false positives in the permutation... #> Differential expression analysis completed. expDef <- expressionDefinition(res, treats)

#> . #> done. #> Available columns in results_raw: #> Warning: ‘>=’ not meaningful for factors #> calcNormFactors has been renamed to normLibSizes #> Removing intercept from test coefficients #> [1] "Computing (M,D) values..." #> [1] "Computing probability of differential expression..." #> Warning: `expect_is()` was deprecated in the 3rd edition. #> ℹ Use `expect_type()`, `expect_s3_class()`, or `expect_s4_class()` instead #> Warning: `expect_is()` was deprecated in the 3rd edition. #> ℹ Use `expect_type()`, `expect_s3_class()`, or `expect_s4_class()` instead #> Warning: `expect_is()` was deprecated in the 3rd edition. #> ℹ Use `expect_type()`, `expect_s3_class()`, or `expect_s4_class()` instead #> Initial number of DE patterns = 2 #> Final number of DE patterns = 2 #> converting counts to integer mode #> estimating size factors #> estimating dispersions #> gene-wise dispersion estimates #> mean-dispersion relationship #> final dispersion estimates #> fitting model and testing #> Estimating sequencing depths... #> Resampling to get new data matrices... #> perm= 1 #> perm= 2 #> perm= 3 #> perm= 4 #> perm= 5 #> perm= 6 #> perm= 7 #> perm= 8 #> perm= 9 #> perm= 10 #> perm= 11 #> perm= 12 #> perm= 13 #> perm= 14 #> perm= 15 #> perm= 16 #> perm= 17 #> perm= 18 #> perm= 19 #> perm= 20 #> perm= 21 #> perm= 22 #> perm= 23 #> perm= 24 #> Number of thresholds chosen (all possible thresholds) = 11 #> Getting all the cutoffs for the thresholds... #> Getting number of false positives in the permutation... #> Differential expression analysis completed. expDef_result <- expressionDefinition(cons_result, treats) deByTool <- listDeByTool(cons_result, rownames(counts), expDef_result)

title: Read table count File and creates a matrix from the given content file. — readCountFile
code: # Example using a small toy count table tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv") write.csv(   data.frame(     gene = c("gene1", "gene2"),     sample1 = c(10, 20),     sample2 = c(15, 25)   ),   tmp,   row.names = FALSE ) counts <- readCountFile(tmp) head(counts) #>       sample1 sample2 #> gene1      10      15 #> gene2      20      25

what: Run differential expression analysis using baySeq — runBayseq
title: Run differential expression analysis using baySeq — runBayseq
text: Performs differential expression analysis RNA-seq count data using baySeq package, applies empirical Bayesian methods estimate posterior likelihoods differential expression.

title: Run differential expression analysis using baySeq — runBayseq
code: runBayseq(   data,   groups = NULL,   sampleInfo = NULL,   pCutoff = 0.95,   fdrCutoff = 0.05,   samplesize = 10000,   seed = 42 )

title: Run differential expression analysis using baySeq — runBayseq
text: data numeric matrix raw read counts, genes rows samples columns. Row names contain gene identifiers. groups named list defining group structure baySeq. two-group comparison, use: list(NDE = c(1,1,1,1), DE = c(1,1,2,2)), values indicate group membership sample. NULL, groups inferred sampleInfo. sampleInfo character factor vector indicating group membership sample (e.g., c("control","control","treated","treated")). Used build groups automatically groups = NULL. pCutoff Numeric. Posterior probability cutoff call gene differentially expressed. Default 0.95. fdrCutoff Numeric. FDR cutoff applied adjusted p-values secondary filtering. Default 0.05. samplesize Integer. Number samples used estimate priors baySeq. Default 10000. Reduce faster execution testing. seed Integer. Random seed reproducibility. Default 42.

title: Run differential expression analysis using baySeq — runBayseq
text: data frame one row per gene, containing: gene Gene identifier (row names data) posterior_DE Posterior probability differential expression FDR False discovery rate estimate logFC Log2 fold-change groups (mean group 2 / mean group 1) DE_baySeq Logical. TRUE gene called differentially expressed based pCutoff fdrCutoff Returns NULL invisibly baySeq installed analysis fails.

title: Run differential expression analysis using baySeq — runBayseq
text: function wraps baySeq workflow two-group RNA-seq comparisons. constructs countData object, estimates prior distributions via getPriors.NB, computes likelihoods via getLikelihoods. Genes called differentially expressed posterior probability DE exceeds pCutoff FDR fdrCutoff. log2 fold-change calculated group means raw count matrix provided reference ; baySeq model fold-change directly.

title: Run differential expression analysis using baySeq — runBayseq
text: Hardcastle TJ Kelly KA (2010). baySeq: Empirical Bayesian methods identifying differential expression sequence count data. BMC Bioinformatics, 11, 422. doi:10.1186/1471-2105-11-422

title: Run differential expression analysis using baySeq — runBayseq
code: if (requireNamespace("baySeq", quietly = TRUE)) {   set.seed(42)   counts <- matrix(     as.integer(c(       rnbinom(200, mu = 10, size = 1),       rnbinom(200, mu = 100, size = 1)     )),     nrow = 100,     dimnames = list(       paste0("gene", seq_len(100)),       paste0("sample", seq_len(4))     )   )   groups_info <- c("control", "control", "treated", "treated")   result <- runBayseq(counts, sampleInfo = groups_info)   utils::head(result) } #> baySeq libsizes #> baySeq getPriors #> Finding priors... #> Warning: The '@replicates' slot is not a factor; converting now. #> done. #> Finding posterior likelihoods... #> Length of priorReps:0 #> Length of priorSubset:100 #> Length of subset:100 #> Length of postRows:100 #> Analysing part 1 of 1 #> Preparing data... #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> . #> done. #> Estimating likelihoods... #> ...done! #> . #> done. #> Available columns in results_raw: #> Warning: ‘>=’ not meaningful for factors #>   gene posterior_DE       FDR logFC DE_baySeq #> 1   NA          2>1 0.2208522    NA     FALSE #> 2 NA.1          2>1 0.3452635    NA     FALSE #> 3 NA.2          2>1 0.3897445    NA     FALSE #> 4 NA.3          1>2 0.4168700    NA     FALSE #> 5 NA.4          1>2 0.4357935    NA     FALSE #> 6 NA.5          2>1 0.4485267    NA     FALSE

title: Execute DESeq2 DGE default analysis is configured to read only kallisto files, and count data — runDeseq2
text: countMatrix original table count abundance data groupName list text, name samples treatment numberReplics number replicate (technical biological) sample controlGroup specific reference level used control (name groupName List) contrastGroup specific reference level need compare (name groupName List) fitTypeParam either "parametric", "local", "mean", "glmGamPoi" type fitting dispersions mean intensity

title: Execute DESeq2 DGE default analysis is configured to read only kallisto files, and count data — runDeseq2
code: set.seed(42) counts <- matrix(   as.integer(c(     rnbinom(200, mu = 10, size = 1),     rnbinom(200, mu = 100, size = 1)   )),   nrow = 100,   dimnames = list(     paste0("gene", seq_len(100)),     paste0("sample", seq_len(4))   ) ) groups_info <- c("control", "control", "treated", "treated") treats <- c("control", "treated") numberReplicsModel <- 2 res <- runDeseq2(counts, treats, numberReplicsModel,   controlGroup = "control", contrastGroup = "treated",   fitTypeParam = "local" ) #> converting counts to integer mode #> estimating size factors #> estimating dispersions #> gene-wise dispersion estimates #> mean-dispersion relationship #> final dispersion estimates #> fitting model and testing utils::head(res) #>        baseMean log2FoldChange    lfcSE      stat       pvalue       padj #> gene49 61.33954      -4.474586 1.358609 -3.293504 0.0009894697 0.09894697 #> gene33 45.38362       4.498825 1.648520  2.729008 0.0063525156 0.17994440 #> gene14 22.34744      -3.063946 1.182995 -2.589991 0.0095978393 0.17994440 #> gene48 20.08076       4.787228 1.859389  2.574624 0.0100349212 0.17994440 #> gene58 26.98364       3.625938 1.411685  2.568519 0.0102134190 0.17994440 #> gene5  24.36280       3.983786 1.562752  2.549212 0.0107966639 0.17994440

title: Execute EBSeq gene Expression analysis — runEbseq

Using "robust" option, EBSeq robust genes outliers genes extremely small variances. title: Execute EBSeq gene Expression analysis — runEbseq
code: set.seed(42) counts <- matrix(   as.integer(c(     rnbinom(200, mu = 10, size = 1),     rnbinom(200, mu = 100, size = 1)   )),   nrow = 100,   dimnames = list(     paste0("gene", seq_len(100)),     paste0("sample", seq_len(4))   ) ) groups_info <- c("control", "control", "treated", "treated") treats <- c("control", "treated") designExperimentModel <- rep(treats, each = 2) toolResult <- NULL toolResult$ebseq <- runEbseq(counts, designExperimentModel, groups = treats) #> Warning: `expect_is()` was deprecated in the 3rd edition. #> ℹ Use `expect_type()`, `expect_s3_class()`, or `expect_s4_class()` instead #> Warning: `expect_is()` was deprecated in the 3rd edition. #> ℹ Use `expect_type()`, `expect_s3_class()`, or `expect_s4_class()` instead #> Warning: `expect_is()` was deprecated in the 3rd edition. #> ℹ Use `expect_type()`, `expect_s3_class()`, or `expect_s4_class()` instead #> Initial number of DE patterns = 2 #> Final number of DE patterns = 2

title: Make expression analysis of multiple tools and return results by tool. — runExpression
text: numberReplics number replicates (technical biological) per group groupName character vector, group/treatment names (e.g., c("BM","JJ")) tableCountPath path csv file contains count/abundance data (local) sepCharacter separator used split csv data (comma tab) rDataFrameCount data.frame/matrix table count; rownames genes colnames samples experimentName experiment name outDirPath output directory (created needed) printResults TRUE, writes per-tool outputs outDirPath fitTypeDeseq2 DESeq2 fitType controlDeseq2 control group label DESeq2 contrastDeseq2 contrast group label DESeq2 methodNormLimma normalization method used limma/edgeR methodAdjPvalueLimma p-value adjustment method limma numberTopTableLimma max number genes limma topTable filterIdKnowseq KnowSeq parameter notSapiensKnowseq KnowSeq parameter methodNormEdgeR normalization method edgeR normNoiseq NOISeq parameter kNoiseq NOISeq parameter factorNoiseq NOISeq parameter lcNoiseq NOISeq parameter replicatesNoiseq NOISeq parameter condExpNoiseq NOISeq parameter respTypeSamseq SAMSeq parameter npermSamseq SAMSeq parameter fdrEbseq EBSeq parameter maxRoundEbseq EBSeq parameter methodDeResultsEbseq EBSeq parameter deNovoAanalysis TRUE, skips KnowSeq (requires annotation) progressShiny optional shiny progress callback

title: Execute NOISeq gene Expression analysis — runNoiSeq
text: countMatrix either matrix raw (read) counts. designExperiment replicate treatment samples. groups text list separated comma, name samples treatment. normParm Normalization method t can one "rpkm" (default), "uqua" (upper quartile), "tmm" (trimmed mean M) "n" (normalization). kParam Counts equal 0 replaced k. default, k = 0.5 factorParam string indicating name factor whose levels conditions compared. lcParam Length correction done dividing expression length^lc. default, lc = 0 replicatesParam argument, type replicates used defined: "technical", "biological" "" replicates. default, "technical" replicates option chosen. condExp vector containing two conditions compared differential expression algorithm (needed factor contains 2 different conditions).

cons_result,   geneNames = rownames(counts),   deList = expDef_result ) if (requireNamespace("UpSetR", quietly = TRUE)) {   upSetPlotData <- upSetPlotTools(     df = deByTool, condition = "Control_vs_Treat",     pathOut = ".", writeData = FALSE   ) } #> Warning: `aes_string()` was deprecated in ggplot2 3.0.0. #> ℹ Please use tidy evaluation idioms with `aes()`. #> ℹ See also
